# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'  # define the version of Docker Compose you are using (default to use a file named docker-compose)  
services:    # add all your services here    
 rabbitmq_service:              
   image: "rabbitmq"          
   ports:                       
   - '5672'                     # RabbitMQ server port  (default is AMQP protocol default, see http for web browser-based management interface)   
     target: 15672             # use the same hostname/port to access your broker from a client app. In development you can set up multiple brokers at different ports by defining them individually in another section of docker compose file with their own configuration, e.g.: "rabbitmq_management: 
   - '5672'                     # RabbitMQ server port (default is AMQP protocol default)     target :15672    environment：      RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=admin，RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASSWORD=passwordrabbitmq: 
   - '5673'                     # RabbitMQ server port (default is AMQP protocol default)     target :15672    environment：      RABBITMQ_ERLANG_HOSTS=${RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_ERLANG_HOST}rabbitmq: 
   - '4369'                     # RabbitMQ server port (default is AMQP protocol default)     target :15272    environment：      RABBITMQ_MANAGEMENT_ENABLED=true，RabbitMQ will enable a web UI at the same host:port where it’s running.
